title: Kali Tiger Reserve
tagline: Kali Tiger Reserve — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Kali Tiger Reserve (formerly Dandeli-Anshi Tiger Reserve) is a protected area and tiger reserve.
overview.description: Kali Tiger Reserve (formerly Dandeli-Anshi Tiger Reserve) is a protected area and tiger reserve. It is located in Uttara Kannada district, in Karnataka, India. The park is a habitat of Bengal tigers, black panthers and Indian elephants, amongst other distinctive fauna. The Kali River flows through the tiger reserve and is the lifeline of the ecosystem and hence the name.

topPlaces.name: Dandeli National Park
topPlaces.description: Dandeli National Park is a protected area in the Western Ghats, India. The park was created in 2007 by merging with the neighboring Anshi Nature Reserve as part of a tiger protection program.

items.note: Dandeli National Park is a protected area in the Western Ghats, India. The park was created in 2007 by merging with the neighboring Anshi Nature Reserve as part of a tiger protection program.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kali Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kali Tiger Reserve is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kali Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (72 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (54 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kali Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kali Tiger Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kali Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Kali Tiger Reserve start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kali Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dandeli National Park.
